volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Rope (Section 4.3.1012 - Section 4.4.1058c)

sections.label: Section 4.3.1012
sections.original: Hinc tu nisi malum frunisci nil potes , ne postules .
sections.translation: You can ’ t get anything from here but harm to yourself , so don ’ t expect it . I ’ m taking myself off .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1013b
sections.original: At ego hinc offlectam navem , ne quo abeas . mane .
sections.translation: But I ’ ll turn aside your ship from that direction , that you mayn ’ t be off anywhere — stop .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1014
sections.original: Si tu proreta isti navi es , ego gubernator ero .
sections.translation: If you are the helmsman of this ship , I ’ ll be the pilot .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1015
sections.translation: Let go of the rope now , you villain .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1015b
sections.translation: I will let go ; do you let go of the wallet .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1016
sections.original: Numquam hercle hinc hodie ramenta fies fortunatior .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you shall never this day become a scrap the more wealthy from this .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1017
sections.original: Non probare pernegando mihi potes , nisi pars datur
sections.translation: You cannot convince me by repeatedly denying , unless either a part is given me , or it is referred to arbitration , or it is placed in safe keeping .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1019
sections.translation: What , that which I got out of the sea —?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1019b
sections.translation: But I spied it out from the shore .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1020
sections.original: Mea opera , labore et rete et horia ?
sections.translation: — With my own pains and labour , and net and boat .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1020b
sections.translation: If now the owner , whose property it is , were to come , how am I , who espied from afar that you had taken this , a bit the less the thief than yourself ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1022b
sections.translation: None whatever .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1022c
sections.translation: Stop , you whip-knave ; just let me learn of you by what reasoning I am not the sharer , and yet the thief .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1024
sections.original: Nescio , neque ego istas vestras leges urbanas scio ,
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; neither do I know these city laws of yours ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.1025
sections.original: nisi quia hunc meum esse dico .
sections.translation: only that I affirm that this is mine .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1025b
sections.original: Ét ego item esse aio meum .
sections.translation: And I , too , say that it is mine .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1026
sections.original: Mane , iam repperi quo pacto nec fur nec socius sies .
sections.translation: Stay now ; I ’ ve discovered by what method you may be neither thief nor sharer .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1027
sections.translation: By what method ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1027b
sections.original: Sine me hinc abire , tú abi tacitus tuam viam ;
sections.translation: Let me go away from here ; you quietly go your own way , and don ’ t you inform against me to any one , and I won ’ t give anything to you . You hold your tongue ; I ’ ll be mum . This is the best and the fairest plan .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1030
sections.translation: Well , what proposition do you venture to make ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1030b
sections.translation: I ’ ve made it already ; for you to go away , to let go of the rope , and not to be a nuisance to me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1032
sections.translation: Stop while I propose terms .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1032b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , do , prithee , dispose [*] of yourself forthwith .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1033
sections.translation: Do you know any one in these parts ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1033b
sections.translation: My own neighbours I must know .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1034
sections.translation: Where do you live here ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1034b
sections.original: Porro illic longe usque in campis ultimis .
sections.translation: At a distance out away yonder , as far off as the farthest fields .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1035
sections.original: Vin qui in hac villa habitat , eius arbitratu fieri ?
sections.translation: The person that lives in that cottage , should you like it to be decided by his arbitration ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1036
sections.original: Paulisper remitte restem , dum concedo et consulo .
sections.translation: Let go of the rope for a moment while I step aside and consider .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1037
sections.translation: Be it so .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1037b
sections.original: Euge , salva res est , praeda haec perpetua est mea ;
sections.translation: Capital , the thing ’ s all right ; the whole of this booty is my own . He ’ s inviting me here inside of my own abode to my own master as umpire . By my troth , he never this day will award three obols away from his own servant .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1040
sections.original: ne iste haud scit quam condicionem tetulerit . íbo ad arbitrum .
sections.translation: Assuredly , this fellow doesn ’ t know what proposal he has been making . ( To TRACHALIO .) I ’ ll go to the arbitrator with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1041
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.1041b
sections.original: Quamquam istuc esse ius meum certo scio ,
sections.translation: Although I know for sure that this is my own lawful right , let that be done rather than I should now be fighting with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1042b
sections.translation: Now you satisfy me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.1043
sections.original: Quamquam ad ignotum arbitrum me appellis , si adhibebit fidem ,
sections.translation: Although you are driving me before an arbitrator whom I don ’ t know , if he shall administer justice , although he is unknown , he is as good as known to me ; if he doesn ’ t , though known , he is the same as though entirely unknown .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1045
sections.original: Serio edepol , quamquam vobis volo quae voltis , mulieres ,
sections.translation: Seriously , upon my faith , young women , although I wish what you desire , I ’ m afraid that on your account my wife will be turning me out of doors , who ’ ll be saying that I ’ ve brought harlots here before her very eyes . Do you take refuge at the altar rather than I [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1048b
sections.translation: We , wretched creatures , are undone .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1049
sections.original: Ego vos salvas sistam , ne timete . sed quid vos foras
sections.translation: I ’ ll place you in safety ; don ’ t you fear . But why ( turning to the SERVANTS ) are you following me out of doors ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1050
sections.original: prosequimini ? quoniam ego adsum , faciet nemo iniuriam ;
sections.translation: Since I ’ m here , no one shall do them harm .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1051
sections.original: ite , inquam , domum ambo nunciam ex praesidio praesides .
sections.translation: Now then , be off , I say , in-doors , both of you , you guards from off guard .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1052
sections.translation: O master , save you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1052b
sections.translation: Save you . How goes it ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1052c
sections.translation: Is he your servant ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1053
sections.translation: I ’ m not ashamed to say yes .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1053b
sections.translation: I ’ ve nothing to do with you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1053c
sections.translation: Then get you gone hence , will you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1053d
sections.translation: Prithee , do answer me , aged sir ; is he your servant ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1054b
sections.translation: He is mine .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1054c
sections.translation: Oh then , that is very good , since he is yours .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1055
sections.translation: Again I salute you .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1055b
sections.original: Et ego te . tune es qui haud multo prius
sections.translation: And I you . Are you he who , not long since , went away from here to fetch his master ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1056b
sections.translation: I am he .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1056c
sections.translation: What now is it that you want ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1057
sections.translation: This is your servant , you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1057b
sections.translation: He is mine .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1057c
sections.translation: That is very good , since he is yours .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1058
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.1058b
sections.translation: That ’ s a rascally fellow there .

sections.label: Section 4.4.1058c
sections.translation: What has the rascally fellow done to you ?
